In Re: Socialist workers Campaign Committee
Dear Mr. Bratten-Moen
I am writing in reference to the Socialist Workers Campaign
Committee and to memorialize the substance of our discussions by
telephone on August 2L , 7990.
Socialist Workers Party candidates will 
be .running for
office 
in the 1990 general elections. Supporters of 
the
socialist 
workers campaign are establishing a Socialist Workers
Campaign Committee. That committee will 
be raising money and
making expenditures in support of candidates endorsed by the SWP
who are running on the platform and program of the SWP,
Such a committee, according to our conversation, would best
be classified 
at this time as a broad support recipient
committee. It 
will 
not be 
a
controlled 
committee of 
any
candidate. Decisions on the kind of materials it prints in
support of any candidates, etc., will 
not be decided by any
candidate and no candidate sha11 exercise any substantial
control or influence 
over 
the 
committee. Naturally, the
committee will consult rrit-h candidates concerning thci.1' ,.1"lr=.
their availability to attend meetings and other events, etc,
Each SWP candidate for elected office in California l,rill
make his//her o]'rn formal declaration of candidacy with your
office 
by filing for 501. As we discussed over the phone/ none
of the candidates plans to raise any money or make expenditures
for their 
individual candidacies at this 
time. Therefore,
instead of filing form 502 and opening a bank account, each will
submit a statement to that effect indicating that they might
receive contributions ,'in kind', in the form of paymenl of any
filing fees or costs for printing a statement of quafifications
for the voters' pamphlet. Those contributions will come from
the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee and will be reported by
the candidate or the committee at the proper tine and in 
an
appropriate form as your office nay require and advise,

August 28, l99O
On behalf of the Socialist workers Campaign committee T
thank you for your patient and considerate assistance and
cooperation in helping us follow the rules and regulations of
the FPPC. Should there be questions in the future, we look
forvard to working with you.
Any vritten correspondence should be addressed to me ctlo of
the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee, 2546 W. Pico Blvd.,
Los Angeles, cA 90006.
Very Trul
Robin Ma i se1
Co-chairperson, Socialist workers Campaign committee
cc: Barry Fatland. Treasurer, SWCC

sections.question_synthetic: How should the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee be classified under FPPC regulations, and what filing requirements apply to SWP candidates who do not plan to raise money or make expenditures for their individual candidacies?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an outgoing correspondence from a legal representative to FPPC staff, not an FPPC advice letter response. It memorializes a prior telephone conversation and does not contain formal Q&A sections. The document appears to be seeking confirmation of verbal guidance previously provided, rather than requesting new advice. No formal FPPC response or conclusion is present in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: How should the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee be classified under FPPC regulations, and what filing requirements apply to SWP candidates who do not plan to raise money or make expenditures for their individual candidacies?

Street Sacramento, CA Attn. : Kevin Bratten-Moen In Re: Socialist workers Campaign Committee Dear Mr. Bratten-Moen I am writing in reference to the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee and to memorialize the substance of our discussions by telephone on August 2L , 7990. Socialist Workers Party candidates will be .running for office in the 1990 general elections. Supporters of the socialist workers campaign are establishing a Socialist Workers Campaign Committee. That committee will be raising money and making expenditures in support of candidates endorsed by the SWP who are running on the platform and program of the SWP, Such a committee, according to our conversation, would best be classified at this time as a broad support recipient committee. It will not be a controlled committee of any candidate. Decisions on the kind of materials it prints in support of any candidates, etc., will not be decided by any candidate and no candidate sha11 exercise any substantial control or influence over the committee. Naturally, the committee will consult rrit-h candidates concerning thci.1' ,.1"lr=. their availability to attend meetings and other events, etc, Each SWP candidate for elected office in California l,rill make his//her o]'rn formal declaration of candidacy with your office by filing for 501. embedding.summary: This is a letter from Robin Maisel (attorney for the Socialist Workers Campaign Committee) to FPPC staff memorializing a telephone discussion regarding the proper classification and filing procedures for a broad-based support committee established to raise funds and make expenditures for Socialist Workers Party candidates in the 1990 general elections.
